title: The Bosonic-Fermionic Diagonal Coinvariant Modules Conjecture
overall_summary: The paper sets up diagonal coinvariant modules DBF_{k,j;n} with k sets of commuting variables and j sets of anticommuting variables, carrying a GL_k × GL_j × S_n action. Its central contribution is the conjectural Diagonal Supersymmetry principle: the Frobenius characteristic of every boson-fermion diagonal coinvariant module is obtained from the stable pure-bosonic Frobenius characteristic E_n by the plethystic substitution q ↦ q - εu. The core technical language is coefficient-stable Schur-Frobenius expansion plus the supersymmetric Schur identity s_λ[q - εu] = Σ_{ν⊆λ} s_ν(q)s_{λ′/ν′}(u). For the original hook-coefficient problem, the most useful point is that, after specializing to one bosonic and one fermionic variable, this substitution kills all non-hook Schur functions and sends a hook s_(a+1,1^b) to q^(a+1)u^b + q^a u^(b+1), so mixed boson-fermion formulas can isolate hook coefficients of the stable bosonic Hilbert series. The paper itself is principally conjectural, but it records several relevant specializations and cited formulas, especially the one-boson/one-fermion standard-tableau formula and Zabrocki's Delta-operator formula.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Proposition 1
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let x = (x_ab) be a k × n matrix of commuting variables and θ = (θ_cd) a j × n matrix of anticommuting variables, with all x variables commuting with all variables and all θ variables anticommuting among themselves. Let R_n = R[x; θ] carry the commuting action of GL_k × GL_j × S_n where GL_k and GL_j act on rows and S_n permutes columns diagonally. Let q = (q_1,...,q_k), u = (u_1,...,u_j), and z be the Frobenius variables for S_n. In plethystic notation, set Ω = Σ_{r≥0} h_r and let ε be the virtual alphabet satisfying p_i[ε] = (-1)^i, so that p_i[-εu] = ω p_i(u). Then the GL_k × GL_j × S_n Frobenius characteristic of the full polynomial super-ring is R_n(q;u;z) = h_n[Ω[q - εu] z] = Σ_{μ⊢n} s_μ[Ω[q - εu]] s_μ(z). In particular, for every partition θ, the supersymmetric Schur specialization satisfies s_θ[q - εu] = Σ_{ν⊆θ} s_ν(q) s_{θ′/ν′}(u), where θ′ and ν′ are conjugate partitions and s_{θ′/ν′} is a skew Schur function.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The paper gives this as a standard plethystic calculation. The character of one column of variables is encoded by the super alphabet Ω[q - εu], which packages symmetric powers in the bosonic variables and exterior behavior in the fermionic variables. Taking n columns with the S_n action by column permutation gives the h_n plethysm h_n[Ω[q - εu] z]. The second equality is exactly the Cauchy identity h_n[A z] = Σ_{μ⊢n} s_μ[A]s_μ(z), applied to A = Ω[q - εu]. The displayed expansion for s_θ[q - εu] follows from the skew Cauchy expansion s_θ[X+Y] = Σ_{ν⊆θ} s_ν[X]s_{θ/ν}[Y] together with the standard plethystic identity that replacing Y by -εu transposes the skew shape, giving s_{θ/ν}[-εu] = s_{θ′/ν′}(u).

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Coefficient bound and Corollary 2.1
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let V be any coefficient-stable GL_k × GL_j × S_n submodule, or stable quotient module, of R_n, with variable-free Schur expansion V = Σ_{μ⊢n} Σ_{λ,ρ} v_{λρμ} s_λ ⊗ s_ρ ⊗ s_μ. Let R_n = Σ_{μ⊢n} Σ_{λ,ρ} d_{λρμ} s_λ ⊗ s_ρ ⊗ s_μ be the corresponding expansion of the full polynomial super-ring. Then 0 ≤ v_{λρμ} ≤ d_{λρμ} for all λ, ρ, μ. In the pure bosonic case j = 0, if R_μ(q) := ⟨R_n(q;z), s_μ(z)⟩ is the coefficient of the S_n Schur function s_μ(z) in the Frobenius characteristic of the full polynomial ring, then R_μ(q) = s_μ[Ω(q)] = s_μ[Σ_{r≥0} h_r(q)].
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Since the category of polynomial GL representations and S_n representations over characteristic zero is semisimple, the multiplicity of an irreducible Schur component in a submodule or quotient cannot exceed its multiplicity in the ambient ring R_n. The formula for R_μ(q) is obtained from Proposition 1 by setting u = 0 and taking the Hall inner product with s_μ(z), using orthonormality of Schur functions in the z variables.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Stable pure-bosonic decomposition recalled from Bergeron [1]
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: For the pure bosonic multivariate diagonal coinvariant module, denoted E_n in the paper, there is a coefficient-stable expression E_n = Σ_{μ⊢n} C_μ ⊗ s_μ, where C_μ = Σ_λ c_{λμ} s_λ. Equivalently, in variables, E_n(q;z) = Σ_{μ⊢n} Σ_λ c_{λμ} s_λ(q)s_μ(z). The integers c_{λμ} are nonzero only if |λ| ≤ binom(n,2) - η(μ′) and ℓ(λ) ≤ n - μ_1, where μ′ is the conjugate partition, η(α) = Σ_i α_i(i-1), |λ| is the size of λ, and ℓ(λ) is the number of parts of λ.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: This decomposition and the stated support restrictions are recalled from the author's earlier paper [1]; no proof is included in this note. The role of the decomposition here is to isolate universal GL_k multiplicities c_{λμ}, independent of the number k of bosonic variable sets, before specializing to any finite alphabet q.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Conjecture 1: Diagonal Supersymmetry
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let DBF_{k,j;n} be the boson-fermion diagonal coinvariant module R_{k,j;n}/⟨(R_{k,j;n}^{S_n})_+⟩, where R_{k,j;n} = R[x;θ] is the super-polynomial ring in k bosonic and j fermionic sets of n variables and the ideal is generated by S_n-invariants with zero constant term. Let E_n(q;z) = Σ_{μ⊢n} C_μ(q)s_μ(z) = Σ_{μ⊢n}Σ_λ c_{λμ}s_λ(q)s_μ(z) be the stable pure-bosonic Frobenius characteristic. The conjecture asserts that the multigraded Frobenius characteristic of DBF_{k,j;n} is DBF_{k,j;n}(q;u;z) = E_n(q - εu;z) = Σ_{μ⊢n} C_μ[q - εu]s_μ(z) = Σ_{μ⊢n}Σ_λ c_{λμ}s_λ[q - εu]s_μ(z). Using Proposition 1, this is equivalently C_μ[q - εu] = Σ_λ c_{λμ} Σ_{ν⊆λ} s_ν(q_1,...,q_k)s_{λ′/ν′}(u_1,...,u_j), and, in variable-free notation, DBF_n = Σ_{μ⊢n}Σ_λΣ_{ν⊆λ} c_{λμ} s_ν ⊗ s_{λ′/ν′} ⊗ s_μ.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: This is stated as a conjecture, not proved in the paper. The expansion after the first equality is obtained formally by substituting the virtual alphabet q - εu into each Schur summand s_λ(q) of the stable bosonic expression and applying the supersymmetric Schur identity from Proposition 1. The paper explains that the conjecture was motivated by the Delta-operator/skew-operator relation in Section 4, especially by the fact that Zabrocki's conjectural formula for DBF_n(q,t;u;z) can be rewritten as E_n[q+t-εu;z] if Conjecture 2 holds. The author reports that experiments confirmed the proposed general formula.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Specializations listed after Conjecture 1
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: The paper lists the following specializations of the generic DBF_n expression. With f^*(z) := f[z/(1-q)], one has DBF_n(q;0;z) = h_n^*(z)/h_n^*(1) for one bosonic variable; DBF_n(0;u;z) = Σ_{a=0}^{n-1} u^a s_(n-a,1^a)(z) for one fermionic variable; DBF_n(q,t;0;z) = ∇(e_n)(q,t;z) for two bosonic variables, where ∇ is the usual Macdonald eigenoperator; DBF_n(q_1,...,q_k;0;z) = E_n(q_1,...,q_k;z) for k bosonic variables; and DBF_n(0;u_1,...,u_j;z) = Σ_{μ⊢n}Σ_λ c_{λμ}s_{λ′}(u_1,...,u_j)s_μ(z) for j fermionic variables.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: These are presented as immediate specializations of the proposed supersymmetric formula and known/cited special cases. Setting u = 0 recovers the pure-bosonic module E_n and its classical one- and two-variable forms. Setting q = 0 gives s_λ[-εu] = s_{λ′}(u), so the pure-fermionic Schur coefficients are the transposes of the stable bosonic GL coefficients. The one-fermion formula isolates the column-shaped bosonic coefficients, while the two-boson formula is the usual diagonal coinvariant Frobenius ∇(e_n).

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Conjectural one-boson/one-fermion tableau formula (Equation 3.14)
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: The paper cites a conjecture of N. Bergeron, Machacek, and Zabrocki that the Frobenius characteristic with one bosonic variable q and one fermionic variable u is DBF_n(q;u;z) = Σ_{a=0}^{n-1} Σ_{γ⊢n} Σ_{τ∈SYT(γ)} q^{α_a(τ)} [des(τ) choose a]_q u^a s_γ(z), where SYT(γ) is the set of standard Young tableaux of shape γ, des(τ) is the number of descents of τ, maj(τ) is the major index of τ, [des(τ) choose a]_q is a q-binomial coefficient, and α_a(τ) = maj(τ) - a des(τ) + binom(a,2).
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: No proof is given in this paper. A footnote states that this formula follows from Zabrocki's conjectural formula DBF_n(q,t;u;z) = Σ_a u^a Δ′_{e_{n-a-1}}(e_n(z)) via a formula of Haglund, Rhoades, and Shimozono. In the context of this paper, the formula supplies an explicit standard-tableau expression for the mixed one-boson/one-fermion module that, under Diagonal Supersymmetry, detects the hook part of the stable bosonic Schur expansion.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Kim-Rhoades two-fermion formula (Equations 3.15 and 3.16)
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: For two fermionic variables u and v and no bosonic variables, Kim and Rhoades show the formula DBF_n(0;u,v;z) = Σ_{a+b≤n-1} u^a v^b (s_(n-a,1^a) ⋆ s_(n-b,1^b) - s_(n-(a-1),1^(a-1)) ⋆ s_(n-(b-1),1^(b-1))), where ⋆ denotes the Kronecker product of symmetric functions and terms with invalid negative indices are interpreted as absent. Equivalently, writing g^μ_{α,β} = ⟨s_α ⋆ s_β, s_μ⟩ and using Frobenius notation (r|s) = (r+1,1^s), the coefficient of s_μ(z) can be expressed as sums of differences g^μ_{(a|b),(c|d)} - g^μ_{(a+1|b-1),(c+1|d-1)}, with a+b = n-1 and c+d = n-1; the paper notes that these differences are known to be positive by Remmel.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The result is cited from Kim and Rhoades [8], and the note does not reproduce its proof. The reformulation in terms of Kronecker coefficients is obtained by expanding each Kronecker product in the Schur basis and comparing coefficients of s_μ(z). The positivity of the displayed differences is attributed to Remmel's formula for Kronecker products of hook-shaped Schur functions.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Zabrocki's Delta-operator formula (Equations 3.17 and 3.18)
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Zabrocki conjectures that, for two bosonic variables q,t and one fermionic variable u, DBF_n(q,t;u;z) = Σ_{a=0}^{n-1} u^a Δ′_{e_{n-a-1}}(e_n(z)), where Δ′_{e_r} is the standard Delta-prime Macdonald eigenoperator indexed by the elementary symmetric function e_r. Specializing t = 0 gives DBF_n(q;u;z) = Σ_{a=0}^{n-1} u^a Δ′_{e_{n-a-1}}(e_n(z))|_{t=0}.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: This is stated as a conjecture due to Zabrocki; no proof is included. The paper uses it as a major motivation for Diagonal Supersymmetry. In combination with Conjecture 2, the sum over fermionic degree a is interpreted as applying a plethystic substitution q+t ↦ q+t-εu to the stable bosonic Frobenius characteristic E_n.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Conjecture 2: Delta via skew
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: For each relevant k, the paper recalls the conjectural identity (e_k ⊗ Id)E_n = Σ_{μ⊢n} (e_k^⊥ C_μ)(q,t)s_μ(z) = Δ′_{e_{n-k-1}}(e_n(z)). Here E_n = Σ_{μ⊢n} C_μ ⊗ s_μ is the stable pure-bosonic Frobenius characteristic, e_k^⊥ is the Hall-inner-product adjoint to multiplication by the elementary symmetric function e_k, and the expression (e_k^⊥ C_μ)(q,t) means that, after applying e_k^⊥ to the symmetric function C_μ, the result is evaluated in two bosonic variables q,t.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: This is a conjecture cited as Bergeron [3, Conj. 1], not proved in the note. Its operational content is that the Delta-prime expression is obtained from the generic bosonic module by skewing each GL Schur component by e_k^⊥ and then evaluating in two variables. The paper observes that the standard plethystic identity Σ_{a≥0} u^a e_a^⊥ f(q) = f[q - εu] converts the sum over fermionic degrees into the substitution by a super alphabet. Therefore, assuming this skew-Delta conjecture, Zabrocki's formula becomes DBF_n(q,t;u;z) = E_n[q+t-εu;z], which directly suggests the full Diagonal Supersymmetry conjecture.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Proposition 2
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: After setting all bosonic grading variables q_i equal to 1 and all fermionic grading variables u_r equal to 1, write DBF_n(k;j;z) for the resulting Frobenius characteristic depending only on the number k of bosonic sets and j of fermionic sets. The coefficient of each S_n Schur function s_μ(z) in DBF_n(k;j;z) is a polynomial in k and j with rational coefficients. Consequently, the total dimension DBF_n(k;j), obtained by replacing each s_μ(z) by f^μ = dim S^μ, is also a polynomial in k and j with rational coefficients.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The proof is the standard evaluation argument for coefficient-stable Schur expansions. In a stable expansion DBF_n = Σ v_{λρμ}s_λ ⊗ s_ρ ⊗ s_μ, setting q_i = 1 for i = 1,...,k gives s_λ(1^k), and setting u_r = 1 for r = 1,...,j gives s_ρ(1^j). For fixed λ and ρ, the hook-content formula shows that s_λ(1^k) and s_ρ(1^j) are polynomials in k and j, respectively. Since the expansion is finite in each n, each coefficient of s_μ(z) is a finite rational linear combination of such products. Replacing s_μ(z) by the fixed integer f^μ preserves polynomiality.

proof_techniques.name: Plethystic super-alphabet substitution
proof_techniques.description: The main formal device is to encode the passage from pure bosonic variables to mixed bosonic/fermionic variables by replacing the bosonic alphabet q with the virtual alphabet q - εu, where ε is chosen so that p_i[ε] = (-1)^i. Under this substitution Schur functions expand by the transposed skew formula s_λ[q - εu] = Σ_{ν⊆λ}s_ν(q)s_{λ′/ν′}(u). This converts stable GL_k coefficients in E_n into simultaneous GL_k × GL_j coefficients in DBF_{k,j;n}.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: The original coefficients c_λ(n) are stable bosonic Schur coefficients after forgetting the S_n Frobenius variable. The substitution gives a way to probe selected shapes λ by adding fermionic variables and studying mixed coinvariant modules.

proof_techniques.name: One-boson/one-fermion hook detector
proof_techniques.description: Specialize the identity s_λ[q - εu] = Σ_{ν⊆λ}s_ν(q)s_{λ′/ν′}(u) to one bosonic variable q and one fermionic variable u. Then s_ν(q) is nonzero only when ν is a row, and s_{λ′/ν′}(u) is nonzero only when λ′/ν′ is a horizontal strip, equivalently λ/ν is a vertical strip. These two conditions force λ to be a hook. For λ = (a+1,1^b), the only contributing ν are the rows of lengths a+1 and a, giving s_(a+1,1^b)[q - εu] = q^(a+1)u^b + q^a u^(b+1). Thus the one-boson/one-fermion DBF characteristic is precisely a two-term transform of the hook part of E_n.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: Under Diagonal Supersymmetry, if H_{r,s,μ} is the coefficient of q^r u^s s_μ(z) in DBF_{1,1;n}(q;u;z), then H_{r,s,μ} = c_{(r,1^s),μ} + c_{(r+1,1^(s-1)),μ}, with zero conventions. Hence c_{(r,1^s),μ} can be recovered by the triangular inversion c_{(r,1^s),μ} = Σ_{i=0}^s (-1)^i H_{r+i,s-i,μ}; after dropping z, the same inversion applies to the Hilbert-series coefficients c_(r,1^s)(n). This is the key mechanism for turning a DBF_{1,1;n} combinatorial formula into a hook-coefficient interpretation.

proof_techniques.name: Dropping the S_n Frobenius variable to obtain Hilbert series
proof_techniques.description: The paper distinguishes the full Frobenius characteristic V(q;u;z) = Σ v_{λρμ}s_λ(q)s_ρ(u)s_μ(z) from the Hilbert series or graded character V(q;u), obtained by replacing each S_n Schur function s_μ(z) by f^μ, the number of standard Young tableaux of shape μ. This is stated explicitly in the global setup as the passage from Equation (2.1) to Equation (2.2).
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: A proof can work at the stronger Frobenius level, recovering c_{λμ}, and then pass to the original Hilbert coefficient by summing c_{λμ}f^μ over μ⊢n. This avoids losing representation-theoretic structure too early.

proof_techniques.name: Pure fermionic transposition
proof_techniques.description: Setting q = 0 in the Diagonal Supersymmetry formula gives DBF_n(0;u_1,...,u_j;z) = Σ_{μ,λ} c_{λμ}s_{λ′}(u_1,...,u_j)s_μ(z). Thus pure fermionic diagonal coinvariants encode the same stable bosonic coefficients, but with the GL partition transposed. The one-fermion formula DBF_n(0;u;z) = Σ_{a=0}^{n-1}u^a s_(n-a,1^a)(z) is an extreme case that isolates column-shaped bosonic partitions.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: For hook λ, the coefficient c_{λμ} is the coefficient of the transposed hook s_{λ′}(u) in the pure fermionic Frobenius characteristic. If a combinatorial model for pure fermionic DBF_n is available in enough fermionic variables, this gives a direct route to hook coefficients; the one-fermion and two-fermion formulas provide boundary and test cases.

proof_techniques.name: Skewing by elementary symmetric functions as fermionic grading
proof_techniques.description: Section 4 explains that applying e_a^⊥ to the bosonic Schur coefficients C_μ of E_n and then summing with weights u^a is equivalent to the plethystic substitution by q - εu. Conjecture 2 links these skewed components to Delta-prime Macdonald operators: Σ_μ(e_a^⊥C_μ)(q,t)s_μ(z) = Δ′_{e_{n-a-1}}(e_n(z)). This gives an operator-theoretic way to compute mixed boson/fermion Frobenius characteristics from the generic bosonic one.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: For hook shapes, e_a^⊥ has particularly simple behavior on Schur functions via vertical-strip removal. This suggests reducing hook coefficient extraction to known or conjectural Delta-operator combinatorics, especially the t = 0 standard-tableau formula cited as Equation (3.14).

proof_techniques.name: Use external combinatorial formulas for special DBF modules
proof_techniques.description: The paper records several special formulas: the one-boson/one-fermion standard-tableau expression involving descents and major index; Zabrocki's Delta-operator expression for two bosons and one fermion; and Kim-Rhoades's pure two-fermion expression in terms of Kronecker products of hook Schur functions. These are not derived in the note, but they are presented as compatible pieces of the same boson-fermion framework.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: The one-boson/one-fermion formula is the most directly relevant: combined with the hook detector, it gives explicit q,u coefficients that can be inverted to obtain hook Schur coefficients. The Kronecker-hook and Delta formulations offer alternative verification paths and possible positive/combinatorial refinements.

proof_techniques.name: Coefficient stability and variable-free notation
proof_techniques.description: The paper systematically separates universal multiplicities from the number of variable sets by writing stable expressions such as E_n = Σ c_{λμ}s_λ ⊗ s_μ and DBF_n = Σ v_{λρμ}s_λ ⊗ s_ρ ⊗ s_μ. The finite numbers k and j enter only by evaluating Schur functions in k or j variables, which may make some summands vanish if the alphabet is too small.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: This matches the original statement that c_λ(n) is independent of m. One can prove or interpret stable coefficients in an auxiliary setting with extra bosonic/fermionic variables and then specialize back to any m.

other_useful_info: Example 5 gives a useful test case for n = 3: E_3 = 1⊗s_3 + (s_1+s_2)⊗s_21 + (s_11+s_3)⊗s_111. Applying the conjectural substitution gives DBF_3 = 1⊗1⊗s_3 + (s_1⊗1 + 1⊗s_1 + s_2⊗1 + s_1⊗s_1 + 1⊗s_11)⊗s_21 + (s_11⊗1 + s_1⊗s_1 + 1⊗s_2 + s_3⊗1 + s_2⊗s_1 + s_1⊗s_11 + 1⊗s_111)⊗s_111. In particular, the one-boson/one-fermion specialization obtained from this example is DBF_3(q;u;z) = s_3(z) + (q+u+q^2+qu)s_21(z) + (qu+u^2+q^3+q^2u)s_111(z), illustrating the two-term hook transform. The paper also records a low-degree conjecture DBF_n(q;0;z) = h_n[Ω(q)z]/h_n[Ω(q)] through q-degree at most n, and a Schur-positivity conjecture involving theta operators from D'Adderio-Iraci-Wyngaerd. References explicitly pointed to by the paper that may matter for a hook-coefficient proof are [1] for coefficient stability of multivariate diagonal coinvariants, [7] for the Haglund-Rhoades-Shimozono formula behind the one-boson/one-fermion tableau expression, [8] for exterior/fermionic diagonal coinvariants, [13] for Kronecker products of hook Schur functions, [16] for Zabrocki's Delta module, and [6] for theta-operator refinements.
why_relevant: Formulates the bosonic-fermionic framework and the conjectural principle that hook and more general super-Schur coefficients can be recovered from mixed bosonic/fermionic diagonal coinvariants. It is the conceptual predecessor to the supersymmetry result now used to reduce hook coefficients to superspace.
